page: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server running on the KVM hypervisor. It features an AMD EPYC 9575F processor with 20 vCPUs operating at 2.0 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The system is equipped with 256.0 GB of system memory, providing 12.8 GB of RAM per core. Acceleration is delivered via a single NVIDIA L40S GPU based on the Ada Lovelace architecture, which includes 48 GB of VRAM. This instance does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met through network-attached volumes.

Benchmark data indicates top-tier single-core performance, with a stress-ng single-core score of 4189.09 and a Geekbench single-core score of 3090.0, both placing in the top 10% of tested systems. Multi-core performance is strong, with a Geekbench multi-core score of 17869.0 and a PassMark CPU score of 62326.31. Large-block memory read bandwidth reaches 309.72 GB/sec, while write bandwidth is measured at 135.97 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the system demonstrates top-tier efficiency in image processing and text processing, alongside strong performance in software compilation and database operations.

In LLM inference benchmarks, the server processes 4434.62 tokens/sec for small 135M model prompts and generates 386.89 tokens/sec. For medium 7B models, text generation reaches 36.85 tokens/sec. However, performance scales down to average levels for larger workloads, with prompt processing for 70B models registering at 14.75 tokens/sec and text generation at 3.97 tokens/sec. Additionally, the CPU allocation is shared, which represents a potential tradeoff under sustained multi-tenant resource contention.

Given the high memory capacity and dedicated L40S GPU, this configuration offers a balanced resource profile for machine learning inference, image processing, and data-intensive applications. The absence of local storage and the shared CPU model represent qualitative tradeoffs that lower the entry cost for GPU-accelerated compute compared to dedicated bare-metal alternatives. It is suitable for LLM inference on small-to-medium models, batch image processing, and parallelized data compilation tasks.
description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-1xL40S is a GPU-accelerated virtual server featuring 20 vCPUs powered by an AMD EPYC 9575F processor, 256.0 GB of RAM, and a single NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB of VRAM. Operating on the KVM hypervisor with an x86_64 architecture, the server utilizes a shared CPU allocation model and does not include local storage. In benchmark testing, the instance delivers top-tier single-core CPU performance and strong multi-core capabilities, alongside high memory bandwidth. LLM inference speeds are highly efficient for small-to-medium models, though performance decreases to average levels for large 70B models. This server is configured for workloads such as machine learning inference, image processing, software compilation, and database operations where GPU acceleration and high memory capacity are required.
og_description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-1xL40S is a KVM virtual server with 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU, delivering top-tier single-core and strong machine learning inference performance.
meta_description: UpCloud GPU-20xCPU-256GB-1xL40S features 20 AMD EPYC vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and an NVIDIA L40S GPU for accelerated machine learning and data workloads.
tagline: An accelerated virtual server combining AMD EPYC processors and NVIDIA L40S graphics for memory-intensive machine learning and parallel computing.
bullet_points: NVIDIA L40S GPU with 48 GB VRAM for accelerated computing.
20 AMD EPYC 9575F vCPUs with shared CPU allocation.
256 GB of system memory providing 12.8 GB per core.
Top-tier single-core CPU and image processing benchmark performance.
Optimized for small-to-medium LLM inference and software compilation.
No local storage included, requiring network-attached storage.
